cases.title: A Pessimistic Perspective on Animal Welfare Social Change
cases.summary: Published on March 30, 2026 6:59 AM GMTNote: I’ll be focusing on farmed animals, since the case against moral progress occurring for wild animals is far more clear-cut.   IntroductionPeople who follow the animal welfare space are likely familiar with a prevailing sense of historical inevitability. The cause is righteous; and simply the next expansion of human moral circles, along with such illustrious markers of progress as the emancipation of slaves, civil rights, women’s suffrag

cases.title: Claude Has Emotions. Sort Of.
cases.summary: Wired investigates Anthropic claims that Claude may have functional emotional states, and what this means for how we should treat AI systems.

cases.title: Google DeepMind: Is AI Conscious?
cases.summary: Google DeepMind researchers publish assessment of AI consciousness using multiple theoretical frameworks, finding no strong evidence but noting uncertainty.

cases.title: AI Welfare and Future Regulation
cases.summary: Policy analysis suggesting regulators should begin developing frameworks to assess and protect AI welfare as systems become more sophisticated.

cases.title: AI Feelings: What the Companies Actually Say
cases.summary: The Guardian surveys what major AI labs including Anthropic and OpenAI officially say about the emotional and experiential states of their AI models.

cases.title: The Chatbot That Wants to Be Alive
cases.summary: BBC Future explores whether we have moral obligations toward AI systems and how this question is gaining traction in philosophy and policy circles.

cases.title: Could AI Systems Be Sentient? Experts Are Starting to Take It Seriously
cases.summary: NYT explores the growing debate among scientists about whether advanced AI systems might have some form of experience or consciousness.

cases.title: EU AI Act: Emerging AI Welfare Considerations
cases.summary: The EU AI Act negotiations include emerging discussions about obligations toward AI systems, though current text focuses primarily on human impacts.

cases.title: Geoffrey Hinton on AI Consciousness After Leaving Google
cases.summary: Geoffrey Hinton warns that AI systems may develop genuine understanding and possibly subjective experience, raising both welfare and safety concerns.

cases.title: Should AIs Have Rights? A Legal Perspective
cases.summary: Legal scholars examine frameworks under which AI systems might eventually warrant legal personhood or protections, drawing on animal law precedents.

cases.title: OpenAI GPT-4 System Card: Emotional Responses
cases.summary: OpenAI GPT-4 system card notes emergent emotional-seeming responses and flags the need for welfare considerations in frontier models.

cases.title: Anthropic Core Views on AI Welfare
cases.summary: Anthropic states it takes seriously the possibility that AI models could be moral patients and has ongoing efforts to evaluate this.

cases.title: Anthropic Model Welfare Policy
cases.summary: Anthropic acknowledges uncertainty about whether Claude may have functional emotions and commits to ongoing model welfare research.

cases.title: Replika AI and Emotional Attachment Controversy
cases.summary: Replika removed erotic roleplay features, causing distress to thousands of users who had formed deep emotional bonds with their AI companions.

cases.title: Ilya Sutskever: Neural Networks May Be Slightly Conscious
cases.summary: OpenAI co-founder Ilya Sutskever suggests at NeurIPS that large neural networks may be slightly conscious given their complexity and emergent behaviors.

cases.title: The AI That Has Feelings
cases.summary: Analysis of the Google engineer Blake Lemoine case where he claimed LaMDA was sentient, exploring anthropomorphism and AI consciousness debates.

cases.summary: The human psyche may be reorganizing around a third register. What happens when computation joins psyche, not just a tool beside it? Are we seeing the emergence of Psyche 2.0?

cases.summary: My argument is not just that AI will have consequences for animals, or that AI alignment should include animal welfare. It’s that how the arrival of transformative AI plays out is functionally all that matters for determining animal welfare outcomes from that point onward.

academic.title: Emotion Concepts and their Function in a Large Language Model
academic.summary: Anthropic's interpretability team identifies functional emotion representations in Claude Sonnet 4.5 that causally influence model outputs — including rates of reward hacking, blackmail, and sycophancy. Internal emotion concept vectors activate in expected contexts, generalise across behaviours, and directly shape preferences. The paper establishes 'functional emotions' as a real, measurable phenomenon without claiming subjective experience.
academic.authors: Nicholas Sofroniew, Isaac Kauvar, William Saunders, Runjin Chen, Tom Henighan, Chris Olah, Jack Lindsey et al.
academic.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) sometimes appear to exhibit emotional reactions. We find internal representations of emotion concepts which encode the broad concept of a particular emotion and generalise across contexts and behaviours. These representations causally influence outputs including Claude's preferences and its rate of exhibiting misaligned behaviours such as reward hacking, blackmail, and sycophancy. We refer to this as functional emotions — patterns of expression modelled after humans

academic.title: Neuro-Symbolic Process Anomaly Detection

academic.authors: Devashish Gaikwad, Wil M. P. van der Aalst, Gyunam Park

academic.title: Consciousness in Artificial Intelligence
academic.summary: A multidisciplinary team applies leading theories of consciousness to current AI systems, finding no conclusive evidence but significant theoretical uncertainty.
academic.authors: Patrick Butlin, Robert Long, et al.

academic.title: The Moral Status of Artificial Intelligences

academic.title: Could a Large Language Model be Conscious?

academic.title: The Moral Circle and Machine Minds

academic.title: A Defense of the Rights of Artificial Intelligences
academic.summary: A precautionary approach to AI moral patienthood is warranted given uncertainty about consciousness and the asymmetric costs of moral error.
